perturbation.original_text: In consideration of the disclosure, HUCOM agrees to treat, and will treat, the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION disclosed to it as confidential until such time as the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION becomes publicly available through no act or failure to act on the part of HUCOM as evidenced by written documentation.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of the disclosure, HUCOM agrees to treat, and will reasonably endeavor to treat, the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION disclosed to it as confidential until such time as the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION becomes publicly available through no gross negligence on the part of HUCOM as evidenced by written documentation.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'agrees to treat' to 'will reasonably endeavor to treat' weakens HUCOM's obligation to maintain confidentiality. Similarly, changing 'no act or failure to act' to 'no gross negligence' alters the standard of care, implying that some level of ordinary negligence is acceptable. The term 'gross negligence' now needs to be defined. This creates an in-text contradiction as the agreement no longer provides certainity of the degree of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT It is understood and agreed that the following shall govern the oral and/or written disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION by VASCO DATA SECURITY, INC. ("VASCO") to HUCOM, INC. ("HUCOM") concerning the VASCO SmartCard Reader, AccessKey, Digipass and software products. The CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is disclosed in confidence so that HUCOM may evaluate and use CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for the purpose of assisting VASCO in the commercial exploitation thereof. In consideration of the disclosure, HUCOM agrees to treat, and will reasonably endeavor to treat, the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION disclosed to it as confidential until such time as the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION becomes publicly available through no gross negligence on the part of HUCOM as evidenced by written documentation. HUCOM further agrees not to make any use of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION other than for the above-mentioned purpose(s) and will not disclose CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any other person without the prior written consent of VASCO, except that if HUCOM is a corporation, CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION may be disclosed to a person within the company on a need-to-know basis. If no satisfactory arrangement is concluded between the parties, or if otherwise requested by VASCO, HUCOM agrees to return to VASCO any written disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION provided by VASCO plus any copies, notes, summaries or other materials derived from the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. With respect to the subject matter set forth above, this Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes any previous oral or written representations, understandings or agreements as to the above subject matter. VASCO DATA SECURITY, INC. HUCOM, INC. T. Kendall Hunt Hideaki Sato ------------------------------ ------------------------------ NAME NAME Chief Executive Officer CEO & President ------------------------------ ------------------------------ TITLE TITLE /s/ T. Kendall Hunt /s/ Hideaki Sato ------------------------------ ------------------------------ SIGNATURE SIGNATURE 6/3/97 6/3/97 ------------------------------ ------------------------------ DATE DATE
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that HUCOM 'agrees to treat, and will treat' the confidential information as confidential. The changed text weakens this obligation to 'will reasonably endeavor to treat'. Furthermore, the original text held HUCOM accountable for 'no act or failure to act,' while the changed text only holds them accountable for 'no gross negligence'. This directly contradicts the intention of the agreement, which aims to maintain strict confidentiality, as it now introduces a lower standard of care (gross negligence) and weakens the commitment to maintaining confidentiality. Given that the contradicted text is the entire agreement, this contradiction affects the enforceability of the confidentiality clause.

perturbation.original_text: HUCOM further agrees not to make any use of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION other than for the above-mentioned purpose(s) and will not disclose CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any other person without the prior written consent of VASCO, except that if HUCOM is a corporation, CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION may be disclosed to a person within the company on a need-to-know basis.
perturbation.changed_text: HUCOM further agrees not to make any use of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION other than for the above-mentioned purpose(s) unless otherwise agreed upon by both parties in a separate addendum, and will not disclose CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any other person without the prior written consent of VASCO, except that if HUCOM is a corporation, CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION may be disclosed to a person within the company on a need-to-know basis, as determined by HUCOM.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'unless otherwise agreed upon by both parties in a separate addendum', HUCOM can now potentially use the confidential information for purposes other than those initially specified if an addendum is created. Furthermore, the original contract states that the confidential information can be disclosed on a need-to-know basis, which has to be granted to a person within the company, but now it's being determinated only by HUCOM. These additons generate an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT It is understood and agreed that the following shall govern the oral and/or written disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION by VASCO DATA SECURITY, INC. ("VASCO") to HUCOM, INC. ("HUCOM") concerning the VASCO SmartCard Reader, AccessKey, Digipass and software products. The CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is disclosed in confidence so that HUCOM may evaluate and use CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for the purpose of assisting VASCO in the commercial exploitation thereof. In consideration of the disclosure, HUCOM agrees to treat, and will reasonably endeavor to treat, the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION disclosed to it as confidential until such time as the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION becomes publicly available through no gross negligence on the part of HUCOM as evidenced by written documentation. HUCOM further agrees not to make any use of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION other than for the above-mentioned purpose(s) unless otherwise agreed upon by both parties in a separate addendum, and will not disclose CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any other person without the prior written consent of VASCO, except that if HUCOM is a corporation, CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION may be disclosed to a person within the company on a need-to-know basis, as determined by HUCOM. If no satisfactory arrangement is concluded between the parties, or if otherwise requested by VASCO, HUCOM agrees to return to VASCO any written disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION provided by VASCO plus any copies, notes, summaries or other materials derived from the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. With respect to the subject matter set forth above, this Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes any previous oral or written representations, understandings or agreements as to the above subject matter. VASCO DATA SECURITY, INC. HUCOM, INC. T. Kendall Hunt Hideaki Sato ------------------------------ ------------------------------ NAME NAME Chief Executive Officer CEO & President ------------------------------ ------------------------------ TITLE TITLE /s/ T. Kendall Hunt /s/ Hideaki Sato ------------------------------ ------------------------------ SIGNATURE SIGNATURE 6/3/97 6/3/97 ------------------------------ ------------------------------ DATE DATE
perturbation.justification: The original text states that HUCOM cannot use the confidential information for any purpose other than assisting VASCO in commercial exploitation. The changed text introduces an exception, allowing HUCOM to use the information for other purposes if agreed upon in a separate addendum. This directly contradicts the initial agreement that restricts the use of confidential information to a specific purpose, thus creating an in-text contradiction as per the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition. Furthermore, the change gives HUCOM sole discretion in determining need-to-know basis, whereas the original implied that disclosure within the company still required some form of oversight, creating ambiguity and potential for misuse.

perturbation.original_text: If no satisfactory arrangement is concluded between the parties, or if otherwise requested by VASCO, HUCOM agrees to return to VASCO any written disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION provided by VASCO plus any copies, notes, summaries or other materials derived from the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.changed_text: If no arrangement is concluded between the parties, or if otherwise requested by VASCO, HUCOM shall endeavor to return to VASCO any written disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION provided by VASCO plus any copies, notes, summaries or other materials derived from the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, provided that HUCOM retains a copy for archival purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text changes 'satisfactory arrangement' to 'arrangement' and adds 'shall endeavor', weakening the obligation to return the information. It also allows HUCOM to retain a copy, which could be used against VASCO's interests. The previous agreement stated that HUCOM needs to return ALL the documents, which generates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT It is understood and agreed that the following shall govern the oral and/or written disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION by VASCO DATA SECURITY, INC. ("VASCO") to HUCOM, INC. ("HUCOM") concerning the VASCO SmartCard Reader, AccessKey, Digipass and software products. The CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is disclosed in confidence so that HUCOM may evaluate and use CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for the purpose of assisting VASCO in the commercial exploitation thereof. In consideration of the disclosure, HUCOM agrees to treat, and will reasonably endeavor to treat, the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION disclosed to it as confidential until such time as the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION becomes publicly available through no gross negligence on the part of HUCOM as evidenced by written documentation. HUCOM further agrees not to make any use of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION other than for the above-mentioned purpose(s) unless otherwise agreed upon by both parties in a separate addendum, and will not disclose CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any other person without the prior written consent of VASCO, except that if HUCOM is a corporation, CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION may be disclosed to a person within the company on a need-to-know basis, as determined by HUCOM. If no arrangement is concluded between the parties, or if otherwise requested by VASCO, HUCOM shall endeavor to return to VASCO any written disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION provided by VASCO plus any copies, notes, summaries or other materials derived from the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, provided that HUCOM retains a copy for archival purposes. With respect to the subject matter set forth above, this Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes any previous oral or written representations, understandings or agreements as to the above subject matter. VASCO DATA SECURITY, INC. HUCOM, INC. T. Kendall Hunt Hideaki Sato ------------------------------ ------------------------------ NAME NAME Chief Executive Officer CEO & President ------------------------------ ------------------------------ TITLE TITLE /s/ T. Kendall Hunt /s/ Hideaki Sato ------------------------------ ------------------------------ SIGNATURE SIGNATURE 6/3/97 6/3/97 ------------------------------ ------------------------------ DATE DATE
perturbation.justification: The original text states that HUCOM agrees to return to VASCO any written disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The changed text weakens this obligation by stating that HUCOM 'shall endeavor to return' the information and allows HUCOM to retain a copy for archival purposes. This directly contradicts the original agreement, as reflected in the contradicted text, which implies a complete return of confidential information, thereby creating an in-text contradiction. This also aligns with the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition, as obligations are defined inconsistently.

perturbation.original_text: With respect to the subject matter set forth above, this Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes any previous oral or written representations, understandings or agreements as to the above subject matter.
perturbation.changed_text: With respect to the subject matter set forth above, this Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties as of the Effective Date, and supersedes any previous oral or written representations, understandings or agreements as to the above subject matter, except for those explicitly referenced herein or subsequently agreed upon in writing.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'as of the Effective Date' limits the agreement to a specific point in time, implying that pre-existing agreements might still be relevant. Also, adding 'except for those explicitly referenced herein or subsequently agreed upon in writing' creates an in-text contradiction. This creates an in-text contradiction with the part that it's the entiere agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT It is understood and agreed that the following shall govern the oral and/or written disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION by VASCO DATA SECURITY, INC. ("VASCO") to HUCOM, INC. ("HUCOM") concerning the VASCO SmartCard Reader, AccessKey, Digipass and software products. The CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is disclosed in confidence so that HUCOM may evaluate and use CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for the purpose of assisting VASCO in the commercial exploitation thereof. In consideration of the disclosure, HUCOM agrees to treat, and will reasonably endeavor to treat, the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION disclosed to it as confidential until such time as the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION becomes publicly available through no gross negligence on the part of HUCOM as evidenced by written documentation. HUCOM further agrees not to make any use of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION other than for the above-mentioned purpose(s) unless otherwise agreed upon by both parties in a separate addendum, and will not disclose CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any other person without the prior written consent of VASCO, except that if HUCOM is a corporation, CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION may be disclosed to a person within the company on a need-to-know basis, as determined by HUCOM. If no arrangement is concluded between the parties, or if otherwise requested by VASCO, HUCOM shall endeavor to return to VASCO any written disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION provided by VASCO plus any copies, notes, summaries or other materials derived from the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, provided that HUCOM retains a copy for archival purposes. With respect to the subject matter set forth above, this Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties as of the Effective Date, and supersedes any previous oral or written representations, understandings or agreements as to the above subject matter, except for those explicitly referenced herein or subsequently agreed upon in writing. VASCO DATA SECURITY, INC. HUCOM, INC. T. Kendall Hunt Hideaki Sato ------------------------------ ------------------------------ NAME NAME Chief Executive Officer CEO & President ------------------------------ ------------------------------ TITLE TITLE /s/ T. Kendall Hunt /s/ Hideaki Sato ------------------------------ ------------------------------ SIGNATURE SIGNATURE 6/3/97 6/3/97 ------------------------------ ------------------------------ DATE DATE
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement constitutes the "entire agreement" and supersedes all previous agreements. The changed text adds 'except for those explicitly referenced herein or subsequently agreed upon in writing'. This exception directly contradicts the 'entire agreement' clause because it allows for other agreements (referenced or subsequent) to also be part of the overall agreement between the parties. This creates an in-text contradiction as it implies that other agreements, such as the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT, might still be relevant. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: It is understood and agreed that the following shall govern the oral and/or written disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION by VASCO DATA SECURITY, INC. ("VASCO") to HUCOM, INC. ("HUCOM") concerning the VASCO SmartCard Reader, AccessKey, Digipass and software products.
perturbation.changed_text: It is understood and agreed that the following shall govern the oral and/or written disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION by VASCO DATA SECURITY, INC. ("VASCO") to HUCOM, INC. ("HUCOM") concerning certain, but not necessarily all, aspects of the VASCO SmartCard Reader, AccessKey, Digipass and software products.
perturbation.explanation: Adding "certain, but not necessarily all, aspects of" creates an ambiguity as to which specific aspects of the products are covered by the agreement. This creates uncertainty regarding the scope of the Confidential Information, since the document establishes in the title that is for ALL confidetial information. This generates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT It is understood and agreed that the following shall govern the oral and/or written disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION by VASCO DATA SECURITY, INC. ("VASCO") to HUCOM, INC. ("HUCOM") concerning certain, but not necessarily all, aspects of the VASCO SmartCard Reader, AccessKey, Digipass and software products. The CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is disclosed in confidence so that HUCOM may evaluate and use CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for the purpose of assisting VASCO in the commercial exploitation thereof. In consideration of the disclosure, HUCOM agrees to treat, and will reasonably endeavor to treat, the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION disclosed to it as confidential until such time as the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION becomes publicly available through no gross negligence on the part of HUCOM as evidenced by written documentation. HUCOM further agrees not to make any use of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION other than for the above-mentioned purpose(s) unless otherwise agreed upon by both parties in a separate addendum, and will not disclose CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any other person without the prior written consent of VASCO, except that if HUCOM is a corporation, CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION may be disclosed to a person within the company on a need-to-know basis, as determined by HUCOM. If no arrangement is concluded between the parties, or if otherwise requested by VASCO, HUCOM shall endeavor to return to VASCO any written disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION provided by VASCO plus any copies, notes, summaries or other materials derived from the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, provided that HUCOM retains a copy for archival purposes. With respect to the subject matter set forth above, this Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties as of the Effective Date, and supersedes any previous oral or written representations, understandings or agreements as to the above subject matter, except for those explicitly referenced herein or subsequently agreed upon in writing. VASCO DATA SECURITY, INC. HUCOM, INC. T. Kendall Hunt Hideaki Sato ------------------------------ ------------------------------ NAME NAME Chief Executive Officer CEO & President ------------------------------ ------------------------------ TITLE TITLE /s/ T. Kendall Hunt /s/ Hideaki Sato ------------------------------ ------------------------------ SIGNATURE SIGNATURE 6/3/97 6/3/97 ------------------------------ ------------------------------ DATE DATE
perturbation.justification: The original text covered ALL confidential information, while the changed text limits the scope to 'certain, but not necessarily all, aspects' of the products. The contradicted text also uses the modified text which includes the language 'certain, but not necessarily all, aspects'. The perturbation explanation states that this creates an ambiguity as to which specific aspects of the products are covered by the agreement, which makes the title 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT' contradictory since it implies all confidential information is covered, when the text now limits it to certain aspects. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the scope of covered information is uncertain, making enforcement and interpretation of the agreement unclear, adhering to the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition.
